questions.question: What is a polynomial?
questions.correct_answer: An algebraic expression with variables having non-negative integer powers
questions.explanation: Polynomials contain variables, constants and whole number exponents.

questions.question: What is a term in a polynomial?
questions.correct_answer: A part of a polynomial separated by addition or subtraction
questions.explanation: Each term consists of a coefficient and variable part.

questions.question: What is the coefficient of a term?
questions.correct_answer: The numerical factor multiplied with the variable
questions.explanation: For example, in 5x² the coefficient is 5.

questions.question: What is the degree of a polynomial?
questions.correct_answer: The highest power of the variable in the polynomial

questions.question: What is a linear polynomial?
questions.explanation: It is usually written as ax + b.

questions.question: What is a quadratic polynomial?
questions.explanation: It has the general form ax² + bx + c.

questions.question: What is the zero of a polynomial?
questions.correct_answer: A value of the variable that makes the polynomial equal to zero
questions.explanation: If p(a) = 0, then a is called a zero of the polynomial.

questions.question: State the Factor Theorem.
questions.correct_answer: If p(a) = 0, then (x − a) is a factor of p(x)
questions.explanation: It links the zeroes of a polynomial with its factors.

questions.question: What is factorisation of polynomials?
questions.correct_answer: Expressing a polynomial as a product of simpler polynomials
questions.explanation: It helps solve polynomial equations easily.

questions.question: What is an algebraic identity?
questions.correct_answer: An equation that is true for all values of the variables
questions.explanation: Identities help simplify and expand expressions.

notes.title: Polynomial
notes.content: A polynomial is an algebraic expression with variables raised to whole number powers only.

notes.title: Degree of Polynomial
notes.content: The degree is the highest exponent of the variable present in the polynomial.

notes.title: Zero of Polynomial
notes.content: A number that makes the polynomial equal to zero when substituted.

notes.title: Factor Theorem
notes.content: If p(a)=0 then (x−a) is a factor of polynomial p(x).

notes.title: Algebraic Identities
notes.content: Identities like (x+y)² = x² + 2xy + y² help simplify expressions.

facts.fact: A linear polynomial has exactly one zero.
A quadratic polynomial can have at most two zeroes.
The degree of a non-zero constant polynomial is zero.
The degree of the zero polynomial is not defined.
